## Highlights

Patch release on top of [0.5.1](https://github.com/memblin/tkc-lvlab-py/releases/tag/0.5.1).
The headline is a **host-wide config file for `createvm`** (#138) so a
bridge-network host stops retyping the same flags on every run, plus a full
cloud-config `user-data` override.

- **Host-wide config (`/etc/Lvlab.yml`).** `createvm` now reads config from
    four layers, lowest precedence first — `/etc/Lvlab.yml` → `~/.Lvlab.yml`
    → `./Lvlab.yml` → `--config` — deep-merged so a higher layer overrides a
    single nested field while inheriting the rest. New top-level keys:
    - **`networks:`** — a per-bridge map of `gateway` / `dns` / `search`, so a
        static `--ip4 vlan10,<ip>` on a bridge fills DNS/gateway from config with
        **no `--gateway`/`--dns` flags**. An unconfigured bridge still errors clearly.
    - **`default_network`** — used when `--network` / `--ip4 NETWORK` is omitted.
    - **`default_vm_username`** — the first-boot account when an image doesn't
        pin its own `username:`.
    - **`runcmd`** — cloud-init commands run on every VM at first boot.
- **Full `user-data` override (`user_data:`).** For control over the whole
    cloud-config document (multiple users, `write_files`, package installs),
    declare a `user_data:` mapping; it replaces the built-in template. Supports
    `{vm_name}` / `{vm_hostname}` / `{default_vm_username}` / `{password_hash}`
    placeholders (an unknown placeholder fails the run before a VM is created),
    appends your discovered `~/.ssh` keys to any the override declares, and runs
    a top-level `runcmd` ahead of the override's own. The key is **`user_data`**
    (cloud-init's term) — if you're porting an lvscripts `users_data:` block,
    rename it.
- **`lvlab up` honors `networks:` for bridge interfaces** — the manifest path
    fills per-interface gateway/DNS from the same layered `networks:` map.
- **`lvlab --version` / `-V`** — prints the installed version and exits,
    uniform with `createvm` and `deletevm`.

See [`docs/one-off-vms.md`](https://github.com/memblin/tkc-lvlab-py/blob/0.5.2/docs/one-off-vms.md)
("Host-wide config" and "Full user-data override") for the full schema and
precedence rules.

## Breaking changes

None. The new config layers and keys are all optional — with no
`/etc/Lvlab.yml` (or none of the new keys set), `createvm` and `lvlab`
behave exactly as in 0.5.1.

## Install / upgrade

```bash
uv tool install --upgrade \
  https://github.com/memblin/tkc-lvlab-py/releases/download/0.5.2/tkc_lvlab-0.5.2-py3-none-any.whl
```

## Supported hosts

Unchanged from 0.5.1 — Debian 12 (3.11), Debian 13 (3.13), AlmaLinux 10
(3.12), Fedora 44 (3.14). See
[`docs-extra/host-validation.md`](https://github.com/memblin/tkc-lvlab-py/blob/0.5.2/docs-extra/host-validation.md)
for the matrix. The host-config + `user_data` paths were confirmed
end-to-end against a real bridge network.

## Also in this release (not user-facing)

- **Security:** black `24.4.2` → `26.3.1` (Dependabot #28, HIGH — arbitrary
    file write via an unsanitized cache filename), bumped in lockstep across
    `.pre-commit-config.yaml` and the dev dependency group.
- Smoke fix (#135): the smoke runner resolves the `lvlab` binary next to the
    running interpreter rather than relying on `PATH`.
- Dev tooling: `black` + `pylint` added to the dev dependency group and a
    `just pylint` recipe (advisory; not wired into CI).

```
git log --oneline 0.5.1..0.5.2
```
